body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:slash-command

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it

opencode ships manual context compaction as in-TUI slash commands: `/compact` (summarize the conversation in place, keeping the session) and `/summarize` (its documented alias / sibling). Both are REPL commands, registered alongside the rest of opencode's slash surface (`slash_commands: true` in capabilities.json). So opencode the agent CAN run one normal turn to populate context, then compact the history in place via an LLM summarization call, then continue a second turn on the same session — the primitive (`context_curation`) the scenario exercises.

### daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store rather than tailing a transcript. A `/compact` turn is an LLM summarization round that opencode records to the SAME session in its store (compaction is in-place, no new session), so it surfaces to irrlicht as an ordinary `ready → working → ready` arc on the existing session — exactly the visible state arc the scenario's User-observable signal asserts (`ready → working` at compaction start; stays `working`; `working → ready` when done). irrlicht's `CompactionState` domain field is dead code (no production writer), so the daemon cannot LABEL the turn as compaction vs. a normal long turn — but the scenario only asserts the lifecycle arc, which IS observable. The daemon is not the blocker.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: slash-command)

The scenario requires the live REPL `/compact` (or `/summarize`) slash command to fire mid-session. The opencode interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh`) drives opencode through headless `opencode run` subprocesses and implements ONLY three step types in its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch: `send`, `wait_turn` (a no-op — `opencode run` blocks until the turn ends), and `sleep`. There is NO `slash` (or `keys`) step type — an unknown step type hits the `*)` arm and aborts the run with `nonzero(2)`. So the driver has no primitive to invoke an in-TUI command.

### Ruled out: no send-only compaction path (not a false gap)

- A slash command with an INLINE argument (`/model <id>`) could in principle be sendable, but `/compact` / `/summarize` take no argument and, more fundamentally, are TUI commands. Headless `opencode run` does NOT process in-TUI slash commands — it stores them as literal user message text. Confirmed live THIS SWEEP with inline `/model` AND `/new`: a `send`-path run of those commands created a session whose stored message carried the slash text as ordinary user content, with no command effect (no model switch; no session reset). By the same mechanism a `send` of `/compact` / `/summarize` would be stored as literal user text and would NOT trigger compaction — there is genuinely no send-only way to reach compaction.
- opencode MAY also auto-compact on context-window overflow, but that is not the manual-compaction the cell asserts, and it is not deterministically drivable via the send-only driver with a small/slow local model (`lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored`) within a bounded recording.

## What would unblock it

Add a live-REPL opencode driver (or a `slash`/`keys` step to `drive-opencode-interactive.sh`) that drives the opencode TUI — e.g. via tmux `send-keys`, the way the claudecode/codex/pi drivers do — so it can issue `/compact` (or `/summarize`) inside a running REPL after a priming turn. The daemon already reads per-turn session state from the SQLite store, so once the driver can fire the in-TUI command the cell becomes recordable: the compaction turn would land as a `ready → working → ready` arc on the same session, matching the spec's lifecycle skeleton (the spec carries only the visible state arc because the daemon cannot label a turn as compaction).

## Confidence

0.85: the driver grammar (`send|wait_turn|sleep`, no slash/keys) is read directly from the live driver source; the headless-`opencode run` slash no-op is proven this sweep with inline `/model` and `/new`; opencode the agent ships `/compact` + `/summarize` per its docs and `slash_commands: true`. Not higher only because the unblocked recording (a live-REPL `/compact` round on one session) has not been captured — that is the developer task this gap defers.
caveats: Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode the agent ships `/compact` + `/summarize`, and the daemon reads per-turn session state from the SQLite store (a compaction turn surfaces as an ordinary ready→working→ready arc). The blocker is purely the driver's missing live-REPL slash primitive (gap:slash-command).

irrlicht's CompactionState domain field is dead code — the daemon cannot distinguish a compaction turn from a normal long turn. The scenario only asserts the visible state arc, which IS observable; the compaction-vs-normal-turn label is not.

recipe-lint.sh DOES catch this gap: the recipe needs a `slash` step type the driver lacks (its `case "$TYPE"` handles only send|wait_turn|sleep), so the lint reports gap:slash-command — this is a missing step TYPE, not merely a missing capability within an existing arm.

sources.note: case "$TYPE" handles only send|wait_turn|sleep; no slash/keys step — an unknown type hits the `*)` arm and aborts with nonzero(2). No primitive to invoke an in-TUI command.

headless `opencode run` does NOT process in-TUI slash commands — both were stored as literal user message text with no command effect; by the same mechanism a send of /compact or /summarize would be stored as literal text and would not trigger compaction (rules out a false gap)

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session state from SQLite, so a /compact turn (in-place, same session) WOULD surface as a ready→working→ready arc — daemon_capability: full
slash_commands: true (opencode ships in-TUI slash commands incl. /compact and /summarize); context_curation: unknown is the primitive-exercised key for this scenario
